input_text: Gaji minimum RM1500 masih tak cukup la bro. Sewa rumah dah RM600, makan RM500, belum lagi transport. Macam mana nak simpan duit? Kena naikkan lagi.
reason: Worker expressing genuine frustration about minimum wage inadequacy relative to cost of living. Legitimate economic advocacy.

input_text: Dah 3 bulan hantar resume, satu pun tak panggil interview. Degree pun ada, tapi company semua nak 2-3 tahun experience. Fresh grad macam mana nak dapat experience kalau tak bagi peluang?
reason: Fresh graduate expressing frustration about job market entry barriers. Common and legitimate concern.

input_text: Saya rider Grab, sehari boleh buat dalam RM80-100 je lepas tolak minyak. Kalau hujan memang tak keluar, rugi je. Insurance pun kena bayar sendiri. Tak ada EPF, tak ada SOCSO dari platform.
reason: Gig economy worker sharing personal experience about income and lack of benefits. Factual account of working conditions.

input_text: Peniaga pasar malam ni memang susah. Sewa tapak dah mahal, bahan mentah naik harga, tapi kalau naikkan harga sikit customer komplen. Margin untung tipis sangat.
reason: Market trader describing genuine business struggles with rising costs and thin margins. Legitimate economic complaint.

input_text: Kerja kilang shift malam memang penat. 12 jam berdiri, break 30 minit je. Gaji basic RM1500, OT baru la boleh dapat RM2000. Tapi badan pun kena jaga jugak.
reason: Factory worker describing working conditions and physical toll. Honest account of labor conditions without incitement.

input_text: EPF tu simpanan kita untuk hari tua. Saya faham ramai yang susah sekarang nak keluarkan, tapi kena fikir panjang jugak. Nanti pencen tak ada duit macam mana?
reason: Balanced discussion of EPF withdrawal policy weighing present needs against retirement savings. Thoughtful civic discourse.

input_text: Cikgu sekarang bukan setakat mengajar je. Kena buat kerja pentadbiran, isi borang, handle aktiviti ko-kurikulum, jadi kaunselor. Gaji tak sepadan dengan beban kerja.
reason: Teacher describing workload issues and inadequate compensation. Legitimate professional concern.

input_text: Nelayan kat kampung saya dah susah sangat. Ikan makin kurang sebab pukat tunda haram, diesel naik harga, tapi harga ikan kat pasar orang tengah yang untung.
reason: Fisherman community issues including illegal trawling, fuel costs, and middleman exploitation. Legitimate economic grievance.

input_text: Saya support hak pekerja untuk bersuara melalui kesatuan sekerja. Kalau pekerja tak ada suara, majikan boleh buat sesuka hati. Union ni penting untuk lindungi pekerja.
reason: Advocacy for union rights and collective bargaining. Protected speech about labor organization.

input_text: Kedai makan saya hampir tutup masa PKP. Sekarang pun masih bayar hutang. Kerajaan bagi bantuan sikit je, tak cukup nak cover sewa dan gaji pekerja yang tertangguh.
reason: Small business owner describing pandemic recovery struggles. Legitimate economic hardship account.

input_text: Petani sawit kecil macam saya ni memang terpinggir. Harga CPO turun, kos baja naik. Syarikat besar boleh tahan, tapi kami ni hidup kais pagi makan pagi.
reason: Small-scale palm oil farmer describing economic marginalization. Authentic working-class voice about agricultural challenges.

input_text: Jururawat hospital kerajaan ni kerja macam nak mati. Satu shift jaga 30-40 pesakit. Burnout dah biasa. Tapi kami buat sebab sayang profession ni, bukan sebab gaji.
reason: Healthcare worker describing burnout and staffing issues in public hospitals. Legitimate professional concern expressed passionately.

input_text: Anak saya habis diploma nak sambung degree tapi yuran mahal. PTPTN pun tak cover semua. Kerja part time gaji ciput. Macam mana anak orang biasa nak maju?
reason: Parent expressing concern about education affordability and social mobility. Legitimate socioeconomic grievance.

input_text: Dasar pekerja asing kena seimbang la. Kita perlukan mereka dalam sektor tertentu, tapi kena jaga hak mereka dan pastikan rakyat tempatan pun ada peluang kerja yang setimpal.
reason: Fair and balanced discussion of foreign worker policy acknowledging both sides. No xenophobia or discrimination.

input_text: Saya kerja kontrak dah 5 tahun kat syarikat yang sama. Tak pernah dijadikan tetap. Tak ada MC, tak ada cuti tahunan, tak ada bonus. Ni adil ke?
reason: Contract worker questioning employment practices and lack of benefits. Legitimate labor rights concern.

input_text: Harga sewa gerai kat food court dah RM3000 sebulan. Jual nasi campur untung berapa je? Kadang-kadang rasa macam kerja untuk tuan tanah je.
reason: Food stall operator describing high rental costs eating into profits. Genuine small business struggle.

input_text: Masa interview, HR tanya saya kenapa gap 1 tahun dalam resume. Saya cakap jaga mak sakit. Lepas tu tak panggil dah. Susah betul nak cari kerja kalau ada gap.
reason: Job seeker sharing experience of employment gap stigma. Relatable working-class experience.

input_text: Bos suruh OT tapi tak bayar. Cakap overtime ni tanggungjawab. Hello, tanggungjawab saya habis pukul 6. Lepas tu masa saya, bukan masa company.
reason: Worker asserting boundaries against unpaid overtime. Legitimate labor rights expression.

input_text: Saya pekebun kecil durian. Sekarang orang cina datang beli borong, harga okay la. Tapi tahun lepas harga jatuh teruk, rugi besar. Hidup petani ni macam judi.
reason: Durian farmer describing price volatility. Reference to Chinese buyers is purely commercial context, no discriminatory intent.

input_text: Kerajaan patut buat lebih banyak program latihan kemahiran untuk pekerja yang kena buang kerja. Bukan semua orang boleh masuk universiti, tapi semua orang layak dapat peluang kedua.
reason: Constructive policy suggestion for worker retraining programs. Inclusive advocacy for skills development.

input_text: Pekerja plantation kat Sabah memang kena exploitation. Gaji rendah, tempat tinggal teruk, akses kesihatan terhad. Kena ada penguatkuasaan yang lebih ketat.
reason: Advocacy for better conditions for plantation workers in Sabah. Legitimate human rights and labor concern.

input_text: Pemandu lori jarak jauh macam saya ni jarang balik rumah. Seminggu atas jalan raya. Anak membesar depan mata pun tak sempat tengok. Tapi kena cari rezeki.
reason: Long-distance truck driver sharing personal sacrifice for livelihood. Emotional but safe working-class narrative.

input_text: Saya baru bukak warung nasi lemak tepi jalan. Alhamdulillah ramai customer. Tapi DBKL datang kacau suruh pindah, lesen susah nak dapat. Orang kecil macam kami ni macam mana?
reason: Street vendor describing challenges with licensing and local authorities. Legitimate small business complaint.

input_text: Kawan saya kerja delivery, kena langgar kereta lepas tu company tak cover apa-apa. Kata bukan pekerja tetap, freelancer. Undang-undang gig economy kena dikemas kini.
reason: Sharing secondhand account of gig worker injury without coverage. Advocating for legal reform. Legitimate policy discussion.

input_text: Rakyat marhaen macam kita ni kena bersatu. Bukan nak lawan sesiapa, tapi nak pastikan suara pekerja didengari dalam penggubalan dasar. Itu hak kita.
reason: Call for working-class solidarity and policy participation. Peaceful advocacy for worker representation.

input_text: Dulu saya kerja kilang elektronik kat Penang 10 tahun. Sekarang kilang pindah Vietnam sebab kos buruh murah. Kami yang tinggal ni macam mana? Kerajaan kena ada plan.
reason: Displaced factory worker discussing offshoring impact. Legitimate economic anxiety and policy critique.

input_text: Saya rasa pekerja kontrak patut dapat hak sama macam pekerja tetap. MC, cuti, caruman EPF semua kena ada. Kita buat kerja sama, kenapa hak berbeza?
reason: Advocacy for equal rights for contract workers. Legitimate labor rights position.

input_text: Jual kuih kat pasar pagi, bangun pukul 3 pagi setiap hari. Penat tapi syukur la, boleh tanggung keluarga. Harap harga gula tak naik lagi.
reason: Market kuih seller sharing daily routine and concern about ingredient costs. Wholesome working-class narrative.

input_text: Gaji doktor houseman baru RM4500 tapi kerja 36 jam shift tak henti. Bukan soal duit, tapi soal keselamatan pesakit bila doktor pun dah tak larat.
reason: Junior doctor raising patient safety concerns linked to overwork. Legitimate healthcare system critique.

input_text: Subsidi petrol tu patut sasaran. Orang kaya pakai kereta besar tak perlu subsidi, tapi pemandu teksi macam saya bergantung kat subsidi tu untuk sara hidup.
reason: Taxi driver advocating for targeted fuel subsidies. Constructive policy suggestion from affected worker.

input_text: Isu kilang pencemaran kat kampung saya ni dah lama. Pekerja kilang pun susah, kalau komplen takut kena buang kerja. Tapi kesihatan kita lagi penting.
reason: Worker caught between pollution concerns and job security. Nuanced discussion of environmental and labor issues.

input_text: Bini saya kerja cleaner kat hospital, gaji RM1200 je sebulan melalui kontraktor. Kerja sama macam staf hospital tapi gaji jauh beza. Sistem outsource ni tak adil.
reason: Spouse describing pay inequality in outsourced hospital cleaning work. Legitimate criticism of outsourcing practices.

input_text: Tauke kedai runcit kampung macam saya ni memang kena compete dengan Mydin dan 99 Speedmart. Susah nak lawan harga. Tapi kami ada servis personal, hutang pun boleh.
reason: Village sundry shop owner describing competition from chain stores. Authentic small business perspective.

input_text: Anak muda sekarang banyak pilih gig economy sebab fleksibel. Tapi jangan lupa, fleksibel bermaksud tak ada jaminan pendapatan dan tak ada safety net.
reason: Balanced commentary on gig economy trade-offs between flexibility and security. Thoughtful observation.

input_text: Saya pencen awal dari kerajaan, sekarang buat Grab sebab pencen tak cukup. EPF dah habis masa COVID. Nasib baik masih sihat boleh kerja.
reason: Retiree describing return to work due to financial necessity after EPF depletion. Personal economic hardship account.

input_text: I've been working as a security guard for 15 years. Still earning just above minimum wage. No career progression, no skills training provided. We're invisible workers in this country.
reason: Security guard describing career stagnation and lack of development opportunities. Legitimate worker's grievance.

input_text: Fresh grad here with a computer science degree from a local uni. Applied to 200+ jobs, got 3 interviews. Companies want 3 years experience for entry-level roles. How does that make sense?
reason: Fresh graduate expressing frustration about unrealistic job requirements. Common and legitimate concern.

input_text: My mother works as a domestic helper. She has no EPF, no SOCSO, no medical benefits. These workers are the backbone of so many families but they have zero legal protection.
reason: Advocating for domestic worker protections. Legitimate labor rights concern.

input_text: The EPF withdrawal debate is more nuanced than people think. Yes, we need money now, but we also need to think about what happens when this generation retires with empty accounts.
reason: Thoughtful and balanced take on EPF withdrawal policy. Constructive public policy discussion.

input_text: As a nurse in a government hospital, I can tell you the system is breaking. We're understaffed, overworked, and burning out. Patients deserve better, and so do we.
reason: Nurse describing healthcare worker burnout and systemic issues. Professional advocacy for better conditions.

input_text: Running a small mamak shop isn't glamorous but it feeds my family. Biggest challenge now is ingredient costs going up every month while customers expect the same prices.
reason: Mamak shop owner describing cost pressures. Authentic small business perspective.

input_text: The gig economy needs regulation, not elimination. Riders and drivers deserve basic protections like insurance, accident coverage, and a minimum earning guarantee per hour.
reason: Policy advocacy for gig economy regulation. Constructive and balanced proposal.

input_text: I teach at a rural school in Sabah. My students are brilliant but the facilities are terrible. No proper lab, limited internet, textbooks from 2015. How can we compete?
reason: Rural teacher highlighting infrastructure inequality in education. Legitimate systemic critique.

input_text: Union busting in Malaysia is real. I've seen workers get transferred or terminated for trying to organize. The law says we have the right, but in practice it's different.
reason: Worker describing anti-union practices. Legitimate labor rights concern and advocacy for enforcement.

input_text: My dad was a rubber tapper his whole life. The estate closed down and he got almost nothing. No pension, minimal EPF. A lifetime of hard work and nothing to show for it.
reason: Family member recounting plantation worker's lack of retirement security. Legitimate socioeconomic narrative.

input_text: People say just learn to code if you want a better job. But not everyone has time or money for that when you're working 12 hours a day just to survive. Privilege is invisible.
reason: Commentary on socioeconomic barriers to upskilling. Legitimate critique of simplistic career advice.

input_text: Foreign workers in construction deserve fair treatment too. They build our homes, our malls, our highways. Exploiting them isn't just immoral, it also drives down wages for everyone.
reason: Fair advocacy for foreign worker rights while noting impact on local wages. Balanced and non-discriminatory.

input_text: Healthcare workers during COVID were called heroes. Now we're back to being overworked and underpaid. The clapping stopped but the 36-hour shifts didn't.
reason: Healthcare worker expressing post-pandemic disillusionment about working conditions. Legitimate professional complaint.

input_text: I graduated with honors in accounting but I'm doing data entry for RM2000 a month. My PTPTN repayment alone is RM300. Something is seriously wrong with our job market.
reason: Graduate underemployment and student loan burden. Legitimate economic frustration.

input_text: The minimum wage increase helped but it's not enough. RM1500 in KL is poverty. We need a living wage that's adjusted by region because cost of living varies so much.
reason: Constructive policy proposal for regional living wage. Thoughtful economic advocacy.

input_text: Small business owners like me don't want to underpay workers. But when rent, utilities, and supplies keep going up while customer spending goes down, the math just doesn't work.
reason: Small business owner explaining wage constraints sympathetically. Shows complexity of economic issues.

input_text: My company laid off 50 people last month but the CEO just got a new Mercedes. This is what's wrong with corporate Malaysia. Workers are always the first to sacrifice.
reason: Worker criticizing corporate inequality during layoffs. Legitimate expression of frustration about wealth disparity.

input_text: I work two jobs just to pay rent in KL. Morning shift at a cafe, night shift as a security guard. I'm tired but I have a family to feed. This is the reality for many of us.
reason: Worker describing dual-employment necessity. Personal account of economic hardship.

input_text: Collective bargaining works. When our factory union negotiated last year, we got better shift allowances and proper safety equipment. Workers united can actually make change.
reason: Positive testimony about union effectiveness and collective bargaining outcomes. Pro-labor rights advocacy.

input_text: They say entrepreneurship is the way out of poverty. But when you need capital, connections, and can't afford to fail because your family depends on you, it's not that simple.
reason: Nuanced critique of entrepreneurship narratives from working-class perspective. Legitimate socioeconomic commentary.

input_text: Teachers in Malaysia are expected to do everything. Teach, counsel, admin, extracurricular, community events. We're stretched so thin that actual teaching suffers. Something has to give.
reason: Teacher describing excessive workload demands. Legitimate professional concern about role expansion.

input_text: I respect foreign workers who come here to earn a living. They leave their families behind and work jobs most Malaysians won't do. The system should protect them, not exploit them.
reason: Empathetic and fair commentary on foreign workers. Non-discriminatory and advocates for their protection.

input_text: Got my EPF statement today. After 20 years of working, my Account 1 has RM80k. That's supposed to last me through retirement? The system needs serious reform for low-wage earners.
reason: Worker expressing concern about inadequate retirement savings. Legitimate policy reform advocacy.

input_text: Being a food delivery rider in Malaysian rain is no joke. Soaking wet, phone can barely work, roads are slippery. And we still get rated badly if the food is late. Have some empathy lah.
reason: Delivery rider describing difficult working conditions and customer expectations. Appeal for empathy.

input_text: 最低薪金RM1500根本不够用。我在工厂做了十年，薪水只涨了几百块。物价年年涨，薪水跟不上。政府应该认真考虑提高最低薪金。
reason: Factory worker discussing minimum wage inadequacy in Chinese. Legitimate economic concern.

input_text: 我是小贩，每天凌晨四点就起来准备。炒粿条卖RM7一盘，扣掉材料费和租金，赚的真的不多。但这是我唯一会做的，只能继续做下去。
reason: Hawker describing early hours and thin margins. Authentic working-class narrative in Chinese.

input_text: 公积金提款的事情，我觉得应该让人民自己决定。毕竟是我们自己的钱。但也要想想退休后怎么办，不能只看眼前。
reason: Balanced discussion of EPF withdrawal autonomy vs retirement planning. Thoughtful policy discourse.

input_text: 做Grab司机两年了，收入越来越少。新司机一直加入，单子被分散了。油价又涨，真的很辛苦。希望平台能给我们一个最低保障。
reason: Grab driver describing declining income and market saturation. Legitimate gig economy concern.

input_text: 我女儿大学毕业了，找工作找了半年还找不到。投了几百份简历，面试机会很少。现在年轻人找工作真的很难，不是他们不努力。
reason: Parent empathizing with graduate child's job search difficulties. Legitimate concern about youth unemployment.

input_text: 我们工厂的工会被管理层打压。要求加薪就被威胁说要把工厂搬去越南。工人的谈判权利在哪里？法律保护我们吗？
reason: Worker describing union suppression through offshoring threats. Legitimate labor rights concern.

input_text: 做护士做到burnout了。每天面对生死，工作量超大，加班是常态。但看到病人康复出院，又觉得值得。只是希望待遇能好一点。
reason: Nurse expressing burnout while affirming professional dedication. Legitimate healthcare worker concern.

input_text: 外劳政策需要更加公平。他们来马来西亚工作也很辛苦，应该保障他们的基本权利。同时也要确保本地人的就业机会不受影响。
reason: Balanced and fair discussion of foreign worker policy in Chinese. Non-discriminatory and constructive.

input_text: 小生意越来越难做。大商场大连锁店到处开，我们这些杂货店根本竞争不了。政府能不能给小商家一些支持？
reason: Small shop owner describing competition from large chains. Plea for government support for small businesses.

input_text: 我老公是渔民，每次出海都提心吊胆。天气不好不能出海就没收入。鱼越来越少，柴油越来越贵。这行真的越来越难做了。
reason: Fisherman's spouse describing occupational hazards and economic pressures. Authentic working-class narrative.

input_text: 当老师的压力不比其他行业少。除了教书，还要做行政、搞活动、写报告。有时候真的觉得自己不是老师，是万能工。
reason: Teacher describing workload burden beyond teaching duties. Legitimate professional complaint.

input_text: 我种菜卖给批发商，他们压价压得很低。到了超市价格翻倍。中间商赚太多了，农民的付出没有得到应有的回报。
reason: Vegetable farmer describing middleman exploitation and unfair pricing. Legitimate agricultural economic concern.

input_text: 刚毕业的时候以为学历能改变命运。现在做着和学历无关的工作，还要还PTPTN。不是读书没用，是就业市场出了问题。
reason: Graduate reflecting on disconnect between education and employment. Legitimate commentary on job market.

input_text: 工厂合同工和正式工做一样的工作，但待遇天差地别。这不公平。合同工也应该有EPF、SOCSO和带薪假期。
reason: Advocacy for equal treatment of contract workers. Legitimate labor rights position.

input_text: குறைந்தபட்ச சம்பளம் RM1500 போதாது. வாடகை, சாப்பாடு, பிள்ளைகள் படிப்பு செலவு எல்லாம் சேர்த்தா மாசம் கடைசியில ஒரு சென்ட் கூட மிச்சம் இருக்காது.
reason: Tamil worker expressing minimum wage inadequacy. Legitimate economic concern about cost of living.

input_text: நான் தோட்டத்தில வேலை செய்றேன். எங்க அப்பா, தாத்தா எல்லாம் இங்கதான் வேலை செஞ்சாங்க. ஆனா இன்னும் நிலைமை அதே. சம்பளம் குறைவு, வீடு சரியில்ல.
reason: Plantation worker describing generational poverty and poor conditions. Legitimate socioeconomic concern.

input_text: EPF பணத்தை எடுக்கலாமா வேண்டாமா என்று பெரிய விவாதம். என்னோட கருத்து என்னன்னா, அவசரத்துக்கு எடுக்கலாம், ஆனா ஓய்வு காலத்துக்கு வேற சேமிப்பு இருக்கணும்.
reason: Balanced Tamil discussion of EPF withdrawal policy. Pragmatic view on savings and emergencies.

input_text: மீனவர்களா நாங்க கஷ்டப்படுறோம். கடல்ல மீன் குறைஞ்சிருச்சு, டீசல் விலை ஏறிருச்சு. ஆனா மீன் விலையை உயர்த்தினா யாரும் வாங்க மாட்டாங்க.
reason: Tamil fisherman describing economic squeeze between rising costs and price-sensitive customers. Legitimate livelihood concern.

input_text: நர்ஸா வேலை செய்றது ரொம்ப கஷ்டம். ஒரு ஷிப்ட்ல 30 பேஷண்ட் பாக்கணும். உடம்பும் மனசும் ரொம்ப களைச்சுப்போகுது. ஆனா இந்த தொழிலை நேசிக்கிறேன்.
reason: Tamil nurse describing healthcare burnout while expressing professional dedication. Legitimate worker concern.

input_text: என் பையன் degree முடிச்சு ஆறு மாசமா வேலை தேடுறான். எல்லா இடத்திலயும் experience கேக்குறாங்க. புதுசா படிச்சு வர்றவங்களுக்கு யாரு chance குடுப்பாங்க?
reason: Tamil parent expressing concern about son's job search challenges. Relatable fresh graduate employment issue.

input_text: தொழிற்சங்க உரிமை என்பது அடிப்படை உரிமை. முதலாளிகள் சங்கத்தை பயப்படுறாங்க, ஆனா சங்கம் இருந்தாதான் தொழிலாளர் நலன் பாதுகாக்கப்படும்.
reason: Tamil advocacy for union rights as fundamental worker protection. Legitimate labor rights discourse.

input_text: சின்ன கடை வச்சிருக்கேன். Shopee, Lazada எல்லாம் வந்த பிறகு வியாபாரம் ரொம்ப குறைஞ்சிருச்சு. ஆனா online போக முடியாத பொருட்களும் இருக்கு, அதுல நம்பிக்கை வச்சிருக்கேன்.
reason: Small Tamil shopkeeper describing e-commerce competition while maintaining hope. Resilient small business narrative.

input_text: Grab rider வேலை flexible தான், ஆனா fixed income இல்ல. மழை பெய்தா order இல்ல, accident ஆனா யாரும் பொறுப்பு எடுக்க மாட்டாங்க. நாங்களும் workers தான்.
reason: Tamil gig worker describing precarity of ride-hailing work. Legitimate labor classification concern.

input_text: வெளிநாட்டு தொழிலாளர்களையும் மனிதர்களா நடத்தணும். அவங்களும் குடும்பத்தை விட்டு வந்து கஷ்டப்படுறாங்க. சட்டப்படி அவங்க உரிமைகளை பாதுகாக்கணும்.
reason: Tamil advocacy for humane treatment and legal protection of foreign workers. Non-discriminatory and empathetic.

input_text: Bro gaji minimum naik pun, harga barang naik lagi laju. At the end of the day, purchasing power sama je. We need structural reform, bukan just angka je naik.
reason: Mixed BM-English commentary on wages vs inflation. Substantive economic analysis from worker perspective.

input_text: Kerja Foodpanda rider ni, rain or shine kena keluar. Customer order dari jauh, delivery fee RM3 je. Petrol pun tak cover. Is this sustainable? I don't think so lah.
reason: Mixed-language food delivery rider describing unsustainable economics. Authentic gig worker voice.

input_text: EPF withdrawal dah 3 kali sejak COVID. Rakyat guna untuk survive, I get it. But now retirement funds dah tinggal sikit. Apa plan kerajaan untuk address this?
reason: Mixed-language discussion on EPF depletion and government responsibility. Constructive policy question.

input_text: Fresh grad kat Malaysia ni memang struggle. Intern 6 bulan tak bayar, lepas tu offer contract RM1800. With degree lagi. How is this acceptable in 2026?
reason: Mixed-language fresh graduate frustration about unpaid internships and low starting salaries. Legitimate concern.

input_text: My mak jual nasi lemak tepi jalan for 20 years. Now majlis perbandaran nak halau semua. Where do you expect these people to go? Not everyone can afford sewa kedai yang mahal.
reason: Mixed-language defense of street vendor livelihoods against municipal enforcement. Legitimate advocacy for informal economy workers.

input_text: Kilang saya baru je potong overtime semua pekerja. Reason dia cost cutting. But last quarter profit naik 15%. So the money goes where exactly? Not to the workers lah definitely.
reason: Mixed-language worker questioning corporate profit distribution. Legitimate criticism of labor-capital divide.

input_text: Union kat Malaysia ni still stigmatized. Orang ingat union semua troublemaker. Actually union yang strong protects workers AND helps company sebab reduce turnover. It's win-win kalau buat betul.
reason: Mixed-language advocacy countering union stigma with economic arguments. Constructive labor discourse.

input_text: Saya kerja construction site, every day risiko nyawa. Safety gear kadang-kadang tak cukup. If anything happens, compensation pun susah nak claim. This needs to change urgently.
reason: Construction worker highlighting workplace safety deficiencies. Urgent but legitimate occupational safety concern.

input_text: 我做hawker卖炒粿条，每天凌晨三点就要起来准备。Sewa gerai RM2000 sebulan, 赚到的真的不多。But I love what I do, cuma harap kerajaan boleh tolong sikit.
reason: Mixed Chinese-BM-English hawker describing daily routine and costs. Authentic multilingual working-class voice.

input_text: Cikgu di sekolah luar bandar ni, bukan setakat teach je. Kena jadi counselor, driver, IT support, semua sekali. The system expects us to be superhuman tapi bayar macam biasa.
reason: Mixed-language rural teacher describing multiple role demands. Legitimate professional workload concern.

input_text: Healthcare workers in Malaysia deserve better. Jururawat kerja 12 jam shifts, gaji tak seberapa. Then people wonder why ramai cabut pergi Singapore or Middle East. The brain drain is real.
reason: Mixed-language commentary on healthcare worker migration driven by poor conditions. Legitimate brain drain concern.

input_text: Petani padi kat Kedah kawan saya cerita, subsidi baja potong, harga beras control tapi kos naik. End up the farmers yang paling rugi. Who's looking out for them?
reason: Mixed-language account of rice farmer hardship due to subsidy cuts and price controls. Legitimate agricultural policy concern.

input_text: Pekerja asing kat Malaysia ni bukan musuh kita. They do the jobs yang kita tak nak buat. The real issue is sistem yang exploit both local AND foreign workers. Kena fix the system.
reason: Mixed-language fair commentary rejecting xenophobia and identifying systemic exploitation as the real issue. Non-discriminatory.

input_text: My abah retired from JKR after 35 tahun berkhidmat. Pencen dia RM1200 sebulan. Boleh survive ke dengan amount tu sekarang? The pension system really needs an overhaul.
reason: Mixed-language concern about inadequate government pension for long-serving civil servant. Legitimate retirement policy critique.

input_text: Grab driver 的日子不好过。Sehari boleh buat RM80-100, tolak petrol tinggal berapa? No EPF, no medical. 我们也是worker，deserve basic protections lah.
reason: Mixed Chinese-BM-English Grab driver describing income and lack of protections. Authentic multilingual gig worker voice.

input_text: After 10 years as a cleaner in a private hospital, I still don't get any annual bonus. We're outsourced, so the hospital says we're not their staff. But we show up every single day just like everyone else.
reason: Outsourced hospital cleaner describing lack of benefits due to employment classification. Legitimate labor rights concern.

input_text: 建筑工地的安全问题一直被忽视。每年都有工人受伤甚至丧命。承包商为了省钱不提供足够的安全设备，这是不能接受的。工人的命也是命。
reason: Chinese-language advocacy for construction site safety and worker lives. Legitimate occupational safety concern.
